original_reasoning: Arabic slipping away and Greek 'to have/hold for oneself' meet through the same holding-versus-loss conceptual axis.
Abiding firmly in a place can drift to a place characterized by what fills it.
Recounting and excelling can both mark singled-out prominence; especially is a plausible abstract result.
Arabic ضئبل 'calamity, disaster' vs Greek 'slanderer, devil' (origin of diabolos). Both negative/destructive concepts; moderate conceptual link
Arabic ghafara = cover/forgive; Greek = Punic Gaphara. Shared substrate for the root cover-hide plausible.
Arabic loss of grip contrasts directly with Greek future passive of 'have', giving a plausible opposite-pole semantic chain.

اقذنّ = produced many faults/defects (أتى بعيوب كثيرة). κίνδυνος = danger, peril, risk. Accumulation of faults leading to dangerous situation is a natural causal chain. Defectiveness as the precondition of danger is a plausible conceptual evolution.
Piled clouds vs 'dry twig' - faint via piled/dried material
